title: Listening - Part 3
description: For each question, write the correct answer in the gap. Write one or two words or a number or a date or a time.
instructions: You will hear one person giving information. For questions 14–19, write the correct answer in the gap.
context: You will hear a woman giving information about a visit to a wildlife park.
audio_script: Woman: Good morning, everyone. I’m going to explain the plan for Saturday’s visit to the wildlife park. We’ll travel there by minibus, and the journey should take about forty minutes. Please meet outside the community centre at [14]9:10[/14]. I know that sounds early, but the park is much quieter in the morning and we’ll see more animals. When we arrive, a guide will take us first to the [15]penguins[/15]. They are fed at ten o’clock, and visitors often say this is the best part of the day. After that, we’ll walk through the forest area. Please wear comfortable shoes, as some paths are muddy. The park has a café, but it is expensive, so I suggest bringing a [16]picnic[/16]. There are tables near the lake where we can eat. In the afternoon, children can join a short workshop about animal homes. They will make a small [17]nest[/17] from natural materials and take it home. Adults can join too if they want. We will leave the park at three thirty, but before that there will be time to visit the gift shop. The shop sells books and toys, but the most popular thing is local [18]honey[/18], which comes from bees kept near the park. The visit costs twelve pounds for adults and [19]seven[/19] pounds for children. Please pay me before Friday so I can buy the tickets online. If the weather is very wet, I’ll send everyone a message on Friday evening. Please also bring a small bottle of water, as the walk through the forest is longer than many visitors expect.

questions.explanation: The guide will take them first to the penguins.
The speaker suggests bringing a picnic.
Children will make a small nest from natural materials.
The most popular thing in the gift shop is local honey.
The visit costs seven pounds for children.
